ÁREAS DE ATUACIÓN

VACANTE 1

Área: SKIN HEALTH

Descripción:

Estamos buscando un/a practicante de Marketing motivado/a y responsable para unirse a nuestro equipo en Skin Health Brands. Esta posición de medio tiempo ofrece una oportunidad única para adquirir experiencia valiosa en la industria del cuidado de la piel, trabajando de cerca con los Brand Managers.

El/la practicante será responsable de dar seguimiento a diversos proyectos, revisar tendencias de mercado y analizar las ventas y la ejecución en el punto de venta (shopper execution). Este rol es ideal para una persona proactiva, con ganas de aprender y contribuir al éxito de nuestra marca.

Actividades:


Apoyar a los Brand Managers en el seguimiento y gestión de actividades de marketing en curso.
Realizar investigaciones de mercado para identificar tendencias emergentes, preferencias de los consumidores y actividades de la competencia.
Analizar datos de ventas e insights de shopper para respaldar las estrategias de marketing y la toma de decisiones.
Colaborar con equipos multifuncionales para asegurar la ejecución efectiva de las iniciativas de marketing.
Preparar informes y presentaciones que resuman hallazgos, insights y recomendaciones sobre el desempeño de la marca.
Apoyar el desarrollo de materiales de marketing.
Participar en reuniones de equipo y contribuir con ideas para la mejora e innovación de la marca.
Realizar un seguimiento cercano para alcanzar el máximo potencial, apoyando el monitoreo de variables clave como distribución, sell-in y participación de mercado.
Apoyar a los gerentes en actividades clave, alineando esfuerzos entre áreas para asegurar el cumplimiento de procesos y la ejecución completa.


VACANTE 2

Área: Marketing Self-Care

Descripción:

Estamos buscando un/a practicante de Marketing motivado/a y responsable para unirse a nuestro equipo de Self-Care. Esta posición de medio tiempo ofrece una oportunidad única para adquirir experiencia valiosa en la industria OTC, trabajando de cerca con los Brand Managers.

El/la practicante será responsable de dar seguimiento a diversos proyectos, revisar tendencias de mercado y analizar las ventas y la ejecución en el punto de venta (shopper execution). Este rol es ideal para una persona proactiva, con muchas ganas de aprender y contribuir al éxito de nuestra marca.

Actividades:


Apoyar a los Brand Managers en el seguimiento y la gestión de proyectos en curso para las marcas de Self-Care.
Realizar investigaciones y análisis de mercado para identificar tendencias emergentes, preferencias de los consumidores y actividades de la competencia.
Analizar datos de ventas, prescripciones e insights de shopper para respaldar las estrategias de marketing y la toma de decisiones.
Colaborar con equipos multifuncionales para asegurar la ejecución efectiva de las iniciativas de marketing.
Preparar informes y presentaciones que resuman hallazgos, insights y recomendaciones sobre el desempeño de la marca.
Apoyar el desarrollo de materiales de marketing junto con el equipo multifuncional.
Participar en reuniones de equipo y contribuir con ideas para la mejora e innovación de la marca.
Realizar un seguimiento cercano para alcanzar el máximo potencial, apoyando a los gerentes en el monitoreo de variables clave como distribución, sell-in, participación de mercado y prescripciones.
Apoyar a los gerentes en actividades clave, alineando esfuerzos entre áreas para asegurar el cumplimiento de los procesos y la ejecución completa.


VACANTE 3

Área:  Shopper Marketing Intern

Jefe: Karen Martinez

Descripción:

El/la Practicante de Shopper Marketing es responsable de apoyar y ejecutar iniciativas estratégicas de marketing para impulsar el engagement del consumidor y aumentar las ventas, colaborando en diversos proyectos y brindando soporte al equipo en la implementación de estrategias.

Actividades:


Desarrollar estrategias innovadoras de shopper marketing para impulsar las ventas y la visibilidad de nuestras marcas.
Colaborar con equipos multifuncionales para la ejecución de campañas de shopper marketing.
Analizar insights del comprador y tendencias del mercado para identificar oportunidades de crecimiento.
Crear experiencias de compra atractivas e interactivas, tanto en canales digitales como físicos.
Colaborar con equipos multifuncionales para desarrollar campañas de shopper marketing que conecten con el público objetivo.
Mantenerse actualizado(a) sobre tendencias de la industria y mejores prácticas en shopper marketing.
Contribuir a la identificación y resolución de incidencias, así como a la mejora del desempeño general de Shopper Marketing.
Investigar e implementar nuevas tecnologías que permitan mejorar la eficiencia en los procesos de desarrollo.


If you are an individual with a disability, please check our Disability Assistance page for information on how to request an accommodation.

title: Remote Events Coordinator
skills: Solution-focused: Think critically and propose solutions independently.
Curiosity: Ask questions and actively seek understanding.
Ownership: Take full accountability and responsibility for your role.
Resourcefulness: Use all available resources to solve challenges or answer questions.
Excellence: Deliver high-quality work and exceptional results.
You have a strong administrative foundation: calendar and inbox management, document organization, contracts, proposals, quotes, and invoices — and you follow through without being chased.
You've coordinated logistics with vendors, venues, contractors, or third-party partners and can manage moving pieces across multiple stakeholders.
You walk into ambiguous situations and create structure. You are proactive and detail-obsessed — you see what needs to happen and move on it before you're asked.
You are warm, professional, and client-ready. You own your mistakes, fix them fast, and think in solutions rather than problems.
Hint: On your first interview, mention which SCORE value you most align with at the end of the interview for huge bonus points.
Minimum of 1 year experience in event coordination, event administration, or event operations, end-to-end, not just day-of execution.
2+ years working with clients from the United States, UK, Canada, or Australia
Must speak and write fluent English
Proficiency with CRM platforms and event management tools; ability to learn new systems quickly.
Reliable internet connection with a backup; ability to work scheduled remote hours consistently. (Minimum: Download Speed +20 MB / Upload Speed 5 MB)
A Computer:
At least 8GB of RAM and an Intel Core 8th generation processor or higher, or AMD Ryzen 2000 series or higher
Operating system: Windows 11 or higher / macOS 13 or higher
Working camera and microphone
Drive the full event pipeline: handle incoming inquiries, prepare quotes and proposals, send contracts, issue invoices, and manage follow-ups.
Be the primary point of contact for clients throughout the event cycle
Coordinate with vendors, venues, contractors, and internal teams to confirm logistics, timelines, and deliverables.
Build and maintain detailed event timelines and run-of-show documents so every stakeholder knows exactly what's happening and when.
Manage and keep the CRM up-to-date: leads, pipelines, client records, follow-up reminders, and event status.
Track inventory, supplies, and equipment needed per event. Stay ahead of what's required so nothing is missing day-of.
Manage inbox, scheduling, meeting notes, and day-to-day administrative tasks that keep the operation running smoothly.
Support business development during slower seasons: lead sourcing, outreach, and light marketing coordination as needed.
Background in a specific event vertical: weddings, corporate, AV/production, food & beverage, or similar.
Experience building internal systems and SOPs from scratch in a growing business.
description:
About the job

About Your Startup Operations

Your Startup Operations is a boutique agency providing operational services to small businesses. We work primarily with small business owners in the United States and our vision is to give power to the people who own small businesses by delivering white glove service while maintaining flexibility, care and affordability.




Our SCORE Values

Solution-focused: Think critically and propose solutions independently.
Curiosity: Ask questions and actively seek understanding.
Ownership: Take full accountability and responsibility for your role.
Resourcefulness: Use all available resources to solve challenges or answer questions.
Excellence: Deliver high-quality work and exceptional results.




Who You Are

You have a strong administrative foundation: calendar and inbox management, document organization, contracts, proposals, quotes, and invoices — and you follow through without being chased.
You've coordinated logistics with vendors, venues, contractors, or third-party partners and can manage moving pieces across multiple stakeholders.
You walk into ambiguous situations and create structure. You are proactive and detail-obsessed — you see what needs to happen and move on it before you're asked.
You are warm, professional, and client-ready. You own your mistakes, fix them fast, and think in solutions rather than problems.
Hint: On your first interview, mention which SCORE value you most align with at the end of the interview for huge bonus points.




Role Requirements:

Minimum of 1 year experience in event coordination, event administration, or event operations, end-to-end, not just day-of execution.
2+ years working with clients from the United States, UK, Canada, or Australia
Must speak and write fluent English
Proficiency with CRM platforms and event management tools; ability to learn new systems quickly.
Reliable internet connection with a backup; ability to work scheduled remote hours consistently. (Minimum: Download Speed +20 MB / Upload Speed 5 MB)
A Computer:
At least 8GB of RAM and an Intel Core 8th generation processor or higher, or AMD Ryzen 2000 series or higher
Operating system: Windows 11 or higher / macOS 13 or higher
Working camera and microphone




Responsibilities:

Drive the full event pipeline: handle incoming inquiries, prepare quotes and proposals, send contracts, issue invoices, and manage follow-ups.
Be the primary point of contact for clients throughout the event cycle
Coordinate with vendors, venues, contractors, and internal teams to confirm logistics, timelines, and deliverables.
Build and maintain detailed event timelines and run-of-show documents so every stakeholder knows exactly what's happening and when.
Manage and keep the CRM up-to-date: leads, pipelines, client records, follow-up reminders, and event status.
Track inventory, supplies, and equipment needed per event. Stay ahead of what's required so nothing is missing day-of.
Manage inbox, scheduling, meeting notes, and day-to-day administrative tasks that keep the operation running smoothly.
Support business development during slower seasons: lead sourcing, outreach, and light marketing coordination as needed.




Desired but not required:

Background in a specific event vertical: weddings, corporate, AV/production, food & beverage, or similar.
Experience building internal systems and SOPs from scratch in a growing business.

title: Customer Service Export Junior
location: Cuajimalpa de Morelos, Mexico City, Mexico (Hybrid)
description:
About the job

 

¡Te invitamos a unirte al equipo Wilson!




Somos una marca líder a nivel mundial que no solo fabrica equipamiento deportivo; creamos las herramientas que definen el éxito en la cancha. 




ESTAMOS BUSCADO NUESTRO PRÓXIMO " CUSTOMER SERVICE JUNIOR "




INDISPENSABLE INGLÉS AVANZADO




ESTA POSICIÓN ES PARA AQUELLAS PERSONAS QUE CUENTAN CON 6 MESES O UN AÑO DE EXPERIENCIA. SE TOMAN EN CUENTA BECARIOS Y EXPERIENCIA EN PRÁCTICAS. 




Objetivo: Gestionar de manera integral la relación comercial y operativa con clientes de mercados de Exportación, asegurando la correcta atención de órdenes de compra, el seguimiento a los requisitos de importación, así como la entrega y la atención al cliente. Actúa como el punto de contacto principal entre el cliente / distribuidor, Planeación y Operaciones. De esta manera, garantizando una experiencia de servicio de excelencia atendiendo consultas, reclamos y solicitudes de clientes en los mercados asignados con rapidez y soluciones efectivas




Requisitos:

Licenciatura en Comercio Exterior, Relaciones Internacionales, Mercadotecnia y/o carrera afín. 

Experiencia: Dos años manejando órdenes de compra, facturación, seguimiento a embarques, verificación de precios, nota de crédito, trato con proveedores. Conocimiento en proceso de exportación.

Indispensable Inglés avanzado

Excel intermdio




Principales Funciones:

· Procesar y dar seguimiento a órdenes de compra de clientes internacionales desde su recepción hasta la entrega final.

· Captura de órdenes en los Sistemas tanto de Facturación LAN, como SAP de US para órdenes Domésticas.

· Verificar la disponibilidad de producto, gestionar backorders y comunicar proactivamente cualquier desviación al cliente.

· Coordinar con los equipos de Operaciones para garantizar el cumplimiento de fechas de embarque de órdenes Vendor Direct.

· Seguimiento a los despachos de bodegas desde USA.

· Facturación de las órdenes de compra de los Distribuidores.

· Preparar y revisar documentación de exportación: facturas comerciales, packing lists, certificados de origen, documentos de aduana y otros según el Incoterm acordado.

· Envío de documentación de embarque original para que los distribuidores.

· Asegurar el cumplimiento de normativas locales e internacionales de comercio exterior dentro de los documentos de embarque de acuerdo a Carta de Instrucciones por distribuidor / cliente, incluyendo restricciones arancelarias y requisitos regulatorios de destino.

· Proceso y Elaboración de Notas de Crédito y Notas de Cargo a los Distribuidores y clientes.

· Mantener contacto con Planners de Wilson US (Team Sports planners) en el caso de Órdenes de Compra Domesticas para solicitar producto.

· Mantener contacto con el departamento de compras para el proceso, seguimiento y embarques para las órdenes de compra.

· Verificación de Forecast /Inventarios en USA.

· Verificación de Precios y/o Costos en PICK al ingresar órdenes de compra.

· Generación de Reporte Allocation sobre ordenes colocadas.

· Mantener actualizada la información de pedidos en los sistemas utilizados PICK, SAP.

· Llenado de Formatos de Alta de Producto.

· Apoyo para la solución de problemas relacionados con embarques, estado de cuenta, consultas de status de pedidos.

· Coordinar con el distribuidor y la parte operativa todo lo relacionado con la entrega de los embarques en tiempo y forma.

· Proceso de Ordenes Especiales (Special Make Ups de acuerdo a necesidades específicas del distribuidor, producto de marketing, etc).

· Elaboración de facturas comerciales (que no emite el sistema) a petición de clientes y/o distribuidores o en su caso por el mismo freight forwarder con la finalidad de poder hacer la exportación.

 

Ofrecemos: 

Sueldo competitivo

Prestaciones Superiores y de ley

Fondo de ahorro

Vales de despensa

Vales de alimentos

Seguro de Vida

Gastos médicos mayores

Contratación directa por la empresa




Zona de trabajo: Santa Fe, CDMX. Esquema de trabajo híbrido.

Horario: de lunes a viernes de 7 a 4 o de 8 a 5.

Communications & Tracking: Monitor and respond to pertinent email communications between clients and external teams.
Campaign Execution: Build, launch, and manage end-to-end paid ad campaigns without assistance across Meta and TikTok (and ideally Google).
Strategy & Reporting: Create strategic and budget recommendations for campaigns and develop regular reports with actionable insights for clients.
Project Management: Ensure meeting action items are carefully noted, assigned, and tracked in ClickUp to ensure timely execution.
Experience: 3+ years of experience building and managing ad campaigns, with deep expertise in the nuances of the Meta ads platform at a micro level, alongside experience in TikTok and Google Ads.
Project Management Tools: Proficiency using in-depth project management platforms such as ClickUp, Asana, or similar software.
Extreme Attention to Detail: Absolute zero-tolerance for typos, inaccurate information, or mismatched event details (such as dates, venues, or lineups).
Communication Skills: Proactive internal and external communication skills with fluent English proficiency.
Team Fit: Experience working closely with a small, fast-paced team while remaining highly organized with your own tasks, priorities, and calendar.
Experience working with ticketed live events, a passion for music, or strong taste in the space.
Hands-on technical experience with tracking pixels (generation, installation, conversion events, and custom audience creation).
Proficiency with Google Analytics to monitor client traffic trends.
Experience drafting and executing email or SMS marketing campaigns using platforms like Mailchimp, Klaviyo, Laylo, Hive, or Community.
Foundational SEO optimization skills across client web domains.
Join a boutique, fast-paced team where your work directly moves the needle for high-quality clients.
Escape the corporate fluff—we focus on meaningful campaigns, proactive insights, and real results that keep clients partnering with us for years.
Opportunity to rapidly expand your operational responsibilities from quality control into full campaign ownership.
description:
About the job

Job Title: Digital Ad Buyer & Quality Control

Location: Remote (Latin America)

Work Hours: Full-Time

Compensation: $2,500–$3,000 USD/month




About The Role

We are seeking a highly detailed and hands-on Digital Ad Buyer & Quality Control specialist to support our performance marketing team. Our client is a boutique digital marketing agency that prioritizes substance over fluff, maintaining a close, high-impact relationship with a limited roster of premium clients across music, entertainment, media, and tech.

This role is structured in phases: initially, your primary focus will be performing meticulous quality checks and reporting across paid marketing, email, and SMS campaigns. Within a month, you will transition into independently planning, setting up, and launching ad campaigns across multiple platforms. If you are an incredibly organized stickler for detail who loves a fast-paced environment, this role is for you.




Key Responsibilities

Quality Assurance: Quality check campaign execution by the advertising team, catching errors in setup, targeting, copy, creative, event dates, locations, and more.
Communications & Tracking: Monitor and respond to pertinent email communications between clients and external teams.
Campaign Execution: Build, launch, and manage end-to-end paid ad campaigns without assistance across Meta and TikTok (and ideally Google).
Strategy & Reporting: Create strategic and budget recommendations for campaigns and develop regular reports with actionable insights for clients.
Project Management: Ensure meeting action items are carefully noted, assigned, and tracked in ClickUp to ensure timely execution.




Must-Have Qualifications

Experience: 3+ years of experience building and managing ad campaigns, with deep expertise in the nuances of the Meta ads platform at a micro level, alongside experience in TikTok and Google Ads.
Project Management Tools: Proficiency using in-depth project management platforms such as ClickUp, Asana, or similar software.
Extreme Attention to Detail: Absolute zero-tolerance for typos, inaccurate information, or mismatched event details (such as dates, venues, or lineups).
Communication Skills: Proactive internal and external communication skills with fluent English proficiency.
Team Fit: Experience working closely with a small, fast-paced team while remaining highly organized with your own tasks, priorities, and calendar.




Nice-to-Have Qualifications

Experience working with ticketed live events, a passion for music, or strong taste in the space.
Hands-on technical experience with tracking pixels (generation, installation, conversion events, and custom audience creation).
Proficiency with Google Analytics to monitor client traffic trends.
Experience drafting and executing email or SMS marketing campaigns using platforms like Mailchimp, Klaviyo, Laylo, Hive, or Community.
Foundational SEO optimization skills across client web domains.




Why Work With Us?

Join a boutique, fast-paced team where your work directly moves the needle for high-quality clients.
Escape the corporate fluff—we focus on meaningful campaigns, proactive insights, and real results that keep clients partnering with us for years.
Opportunity to rapidly expand your operational responsibilities from quality control into full campaign ownership.

title: Junior Marketing & Campaign Coordinator
skills: Mentorship from experienced marketers and direct access to senior leadership
Hands-on work with enterprise platforms (Twilio, SendGrid) from day one
A culture that rewards curiosity, precision, and ownership
Flexible remote or hybrid work arrangement
A real growth path toward a full Lifecycle Marketing Specialist or Marketing Analyst role
Assist in building, scheduling, QAing, and launching email and SMS campaigns using Twilio and SendGrid under senior supervision
Help set up and QA automated lifecycle flows including onboarding sequences, re-engagement campaigns, retention triggers, and other lifecycle marketing initiatives
Support the management of audience lists, segmentation criteria, suppression files, and workflow documentation
Assist in preparing and formatting email templates in collaboration with the design team
Coordinate campaign timelines, internal deliverables, and marketing calendars to ensure campaigns launch on schedule
Maintain organized libraries of marketing assets, templates, and campaign documentation
Support A/B test setup and help document results for subject lines, CTAs, and content variants
Assist with campaign testing, tracking, and reporting across email and SMS channels
Compile and maintain performance reports tracking key metrics including open rates, click-through rates, conversions, engagement, and unsubscribes
Assist in identifying trends in campaign data and flagging opportunities for optimization and process improvements
Help ensure all campaigns comply with applicable regulations including CASL, CAN-SPAM, and GDPR
Assist with list hygiene tasks including managing bounces, opt-outs, and suppression lists
Maintain documentation of campaign workflows, platform settings, operational processes, and process guides
Support ongoing improvements to marketing processes and operational workflows
Work closely with the Director of Marketing and senior team members to align on campaign priorities and goals
Coordinate with marketing, design, and content teams to ensure campaigns are executed accurately, assets are delivered on time, and messaging remains on brand
Participate in team meetings, contribute ideas, and ask questions
Up to 1-2 years of experience in email marketing, digital marketing, or related roles (internships and relevant coursework count)
Basic familiarity with email marketing concepts: deliverability, segmentation, open rates, click-through rates
Comfortable working with data and digital tools; not afraid of dashboards or spreadsheets
Strong attention to detail: you notice what others miss and take quality seriously
Great written communication skills in English
Eagerness to learn marketing platforms and grow into a technical marketing role
Fluency in English (written and spoken). Spanish is a strong asset
Any hands-on experience with an email platform (Mailchimp, HubSpot, Klaviyo, SendGrid, or similar)
Exposure to SMS marketing or basic understanding of how SMS campaigns work
Familiarity with A/B testing concepts
Basic HTML knowledge or willingness to learn email templating
Studies or coursework in marketing, communications, business, or related fields
Any experience in digital, tech, or startup environments
description:
About the job

We are one of the fastest-growing private youth sports operators in the GTA and Florida. We delivery high-quality, structured sports programming across multiple locations, with a focus on fun, skills development, and exceptional customer experiences.

This is an entry-level role for someone who has foundational knowledge of email or digital marketing and wants to develop deep expertise in lifecycle campaigns, automation, and data-driven optimization. You will work side by side with the Director of Marketing, building real skills in platforms like Twilio and SendGrid while contributing to campaigns that directly impact customer acquisition and retention.

If you are analytical, curious, detail-oriented, and excited about how data and messaging come together to drive results — we want to meet you.

WHY JOIN US

We are a growing company in the sports industry, and our marketing team is at the core of our expansion. This role is an opportunity to go beyond sending emails — you will learn the strategy, the technology, and the data behind lifecycle marketing at a company that moves fast and values results.


Mentorship from experienced marketers and direct access to senior leadership
Hands-on work with enterprise platforms (Twilio, SendGrid) from day one
A culture that rewards curiosity, precision, and ownership
Flexible remote or hybrid work arrangement
A real growth path toward a full Lifecycle Marketing Specialist or Marketing Analyst role


Key Responsibilities

Campaign Execution & Support


Assist in building, scheduling, QAing, and launching email and SMS campaigns using Twilio and SendGrid under senior supervision
Help set up and QA automated lifecycle flows including onboarding sequences, re-engagement campaigns, retention triggers, and other lifecycle marketing initiatives
Support the management of audience lists, segmentation criteria, suppression files, and workflow documentation
Assist in preparing and formatting email templates in collaboration with the design team
Coordinate campaign timelines, internal deliverables, and marketing calendars to ensure campaigns launch on schedule
Maintain organized libraries of marketing assets, templates, and campaign documentation


Testing & Performance Tracking


Support A/B test setup and help document results for subject lines, CTAs, and content variants
Assist with campaign testing, tracking, and reporting across email and SMS channels
Compile and maintain performance reports tracking key metrics including open rates, click-through rates, conversions, engagement, and unsubscribes
Assist in identifying trends in campaign data and flagging opportunities for optimization and process improvements


Compliance & Operations


Help ensure all campaigns comply with applicable regulations including CASL, CAN-SPAM, and GDPR
Assist with list hygiene tasks including managing bounces, opt-outs, and suppression lists
Maintain documentation of campaign workflows, platform settings, operational processes, and process guides
Support ongoing improvements to marketing processes and operational workflows


Collaboration


Work closely with the Director of Marketing and senior team members to align on campaign priorities and goals
Coordinate with marketing, design, and content teams to ensure campaigns are executed accurately, assets are delivered on time, and messaging remains on brand
Participate in team meetings, contribute ideas, and ask questions


Requirements

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS


Up to 1-2 years of experience in email marketing, digital marketing, or related roles (internships and relevant coursework count)
Basic familiarity with email marketing concepts: deliverability, segmentation, open rates, click-through rates
Comfortable working with data and digital tools; not afraid of dashboards or spreadsheets
Strong attention to detail: you notice what others miss and take quality seriously
Great written communication skills in English
Eagerness to learn marketing platforms and grow into a technical marketing role
Fluency in English (written and spoken). Spanish is a strong asset


Preferred Skills & Experience


Any hands-on experience with an email platform (Mailchimp, HubSpot, Klaviyo, SendGrid, or similar)
Exposure to SMS marketing or basic understanding of how SMS campaigns work
Familiarity with A/B testing concepts
Basic HTML knowledge or willingness to learn email templating
Studies or coursework in marketing, communications, business, or related fields
Any experience in digital, tech, or startup environments


Benefits

Compensation: $800-1200 USD p/m

title: Remote Internship - Shopify Agency Alliances
description:
About the job

Job Type: Internship




Job Location: Mexico (Remote)




Compensation: Commission-Based (no fixed stipend)




Company Description

Axentra OS is an innovative platform created to eliminate inefficiencies in Shopify stores by automating complexities and streamlining operations. It offers an all-in-one operating system that integrates 33 applications (more are being added) such as logins, customer accounts, order management, exchanges, returns, refunds, reviews, referrals, subscriptions, CRM, engagement tools, revenue generation, analytics, and more. Designed for operational control and scalable growth, Axentra OS provides seamless workflows and refined customer experiences. It helps merchants overcome the fragmentation in e-commerce with beautiful, intuitive interfaces and merchant-centric solutions.




More about us here — https://www.axentraos.com

and here — https://apps.shopify.com/axentra-customer-command-centre




With us, you’ll learn the fundamentals of how modern commerce moves. You’ll connect with founders, marketers, and decision-makers building the next generation of e-commerce brands. You’ll see how conversations turn into strategy, and how technology becomes the edge that defines success.




What you will do

Establish Partnerships with Web Development agencies or freelancers focused on the Shopify platform: The primary focus of this role is to reach out to Shopify merchants and agencies in Mexico via cold calling to establish partnerships between our company and them.




Requirements

• Currently pursuing or recently completed a degree in Marketing, Communications, Business, or related fields.

• You may be a Shopify Developer looking to partner with a leading tech company.

• Excellent communication skills for engaging diverse audiences.

• Strong passion for digital marketing, social media marketing, web development, SEO and eagerness to learn within a startup environment.

• Familiarity with social media platforms, particularly LinkedIn, Facebook, Reddit, and Instagram.

• Proactive mindset, ability to take initiative, and thrive in a fast-paced environment.

• Team collaboration skills coupled with independence and self-motivation.

Compile and maintain performance reports tracking key metrics including open rates, click-through rates, conversions, engagement, and unsubscribes
Assist in identifying trends in campaign data and flagging opportunities for optimization and process improvements


Compliance & Operations


Help ensure all campaigns comply with applicable regulations including CASL, CAN-SPAM, and GDPR
Assist with list hygiene tasks including managing bounces, opt-outs, and suppression lists
Maintain documentation of campaign workflows, platform settings, operational processes, and process guides
Support ongoing improvements to marketing processes and operational workflows


Collaboration


Work closely with the Director of Marketing and senior team members to align on campaign priorities and goals
Coordinate with marketing, design, and content teams to ensure campaigns are executed accurately, assets are delivered on time, and messaging remains on brand
Participate in team meetings, contribute ideas, and ask questions


Requirements

REQUIRED QUALIFICATIONS


Up to 1-2 years of experience in email marketing, digital marketing, or related roles (internships and relevant coursework count)
Basic familiarity with email marketing concepts: deliverability, segmentation, open rates, click-through rates
Comfortable working with data and digital tools; not afraid of dashboards or spreadsheets
Strong attention to detail: you notice what others miss and take quality seriously
Great written communication skills in English
Eagerness to learn marketing platforms and grow into a technical marketing role
Fluency in English (written and spoken). Spanish is a strong asset


Preferred Skills & Experience


Any hands-on experience with an email platform (Mailchimp, HubSpot, Klaviyo, SendGrid, or similar)
Exposure to SMS marketing or basic understanding of how SMS campaigns work
Familiarity with A/B testing concepts
Basic HTML knowledge or willingness to learn email templating
Studies or coursework in marketing, communications, business, or related fields
Any experience in digital, tech, or startup environments


Benefits

Compensation: $800-1200 USD p/m

title: Business Campaign Strategy Intern
skills: Analyze campaign performance data and generate weekly dashboards and insights that identify trends, anomalies, and optimization opportunities, contributing to a 10–20% improvement in campaign KPIs.

Support the planning and execution of cross-channel acquisition and retention campaigns (in-app, push, SMS, paid social), coordinating with product, operations, and creative teams to ensure timely launches and consistent messaging.

description:
About the job

About The Company

DiDi Global Inc. is the world’s leading mobility technology platform. It offers a wide range of app-based services across markets including Asia-Pacific, Latin America and Africa, including ride hailing, taxi hailing, chauffeur, hitch and other forms of shared mobility as well as auto solutions, food delivery, intra-city freight, and financial services.

DiDi provides car owners, drivers, and delivery partners with flexible work and income opportunities. It is committed to collaborating with policymakers, the taxi industry, the automobile industry and the communities to solve the world’s transportation, environmental and employment challenges through the use of AI technology and localized smart transportation innovations. DiDi strives to create better life experiences and greater social value, by building a safe, inclusive and sustainable transportation and local services ecosystem for cities of the future.

For more information, please visit: www.didiglobal.com/news

About The Team/role

As a BC (Business Campaign) Campaign Strategy Intern at DiDi, you will support the design, execution, and performance analysis of marketing and activation campaigns that drive driver and rider acquisition, retention, and platform engagement across multiple regions. Your work will directly contribute to measurable increases in user activation rates, cost-per-acquisition improvement, and campaign ROI while helping the campaign strategy team scale targeted programs across DiDi’s large, multi-product ecosystem.

In this role, you'll be...


Analyze campaign performance data and generate weekly dashboards and insights that identify trends, anomalies, and optimization opportunities, contributing to a 10–20% improvement in campaign KPIs.
Support the planning and execution of cross-channel acquisition and retention campaigns (in-app, push, SMS, paid social), coordinating with product, operations, and creative teams to ensure timely launches and consistent messaging.
Build and maintain audience segmentation and targeting logic using customer and driver behavioral data to improve precision targeting and lift conversion rates by testing new segment strategies.
Develop A/B test plans and track results for creative, channel mix, and incentive structures, documenting learnings and recommending scalable changes to reduce CPA and increase LTV.
Prepare concise business-facing reports and presentation decks that translate campaign findings into actionable recommendations for regional managers and senior stakeholders.
Monitor campaign budget spend and pacing, flagging risks and proposing reallocation strategies to maximize ROI while ensuring compliance with local market guidelines.
Assist in local market research and competitive benchmarking to adapt global campaign frameworks to regional behaviors and regulatory constraints, ensuring relevance and compliance.



We're eager to be in touch because you have...


Last years of bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Business, Economics, Data Analytics, or a related field is preferred.
Strong analytical skills with hands-on experience using Excel, SQL, or analytics tools (e.g., Google Analytics, Looker, Tableau) to manipulate datasets and derive actionable insights.
Proactive, collaborative attitude with experience working in cross-functional teams and the adaptability to prioritize tasks in a fast-paced, high-volume environment.
Attention to detail and a strong sense of ownership, able to manage campaign tracking, budget pacing, and documentation for multiple regional pilots concurrently.



You'll love working at DiDi because...

We create user value

We strive to always create valuable experiences for our users in everything we do. Our focus is to always innovate new experiences that are safe, pleasant and efficient.

We are data-driven

We are strong believers in making informed decisions, that’s why we are data-driven. We can better navigate the business landscape strategically by analyzing valuable metrics.

Win-win Collaboration

Success is a team sport. When we work to help our partners and colleagues win, we win, too. While keeping everyone's best interest at heart, we communicate with candor and execute with excellence in all we do.

We believe in integrity

Integrity is at the very core of our business. We are people who always want to do the right thing. Our intentions are sincere, we speak our minds and listen to each other.

Growth

We always strive to do better. That means venturing beyond our comfort zones, learning from our mistakes, and helping each other grow.

Diversity and Inclusion

Diversity is one of our biggest strengths. Our differences are what make us distinct. We respect each other and believe in equal opportunities for all.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams

At DiDi, we believe that our differences are our biggest source of strength. That’s why we are committed to promoting equal opportunities to all candidates and employees as an Equal Opportunity Employer.

Employment and advancement decisions at DiDi are always made based on the needs of the position and the qualifications of the candidate. We do not discriminate against any employee or applicant based on their gender, age, sexual orientation, nationality, marital status, pregnancy/maternity, disability, race, religion and beliefs, or any other status protected by applicable laws wherever we operate.

We are committed to building inclusive and diverse teams, and a workplace that is free from discrimination and harassment, because that’s how we create better products and services, make better decisions and better serve the communities we’re a part of.

description:
About the job

Job Title

Marketing Data Analyst

Job Description

We're Concentrix. The intelligent transformation partner. Solution-focused. Tech-powered. Intelligence-fueled.

The global technology and services leader that powers the world’s best brands, today and into the future. We’re solution-focused, tech-powered, intelligence-fueled. With unique data and insights, deep industry expertise, and advanced technology solutions, we’re the intelligent transformation partner that powers a world that works, helping companies become refreshingly simple to work, interact, and transact with. We shape new game-changing careers in over 70 countries, attracting the best talent.

The Concentrix Technical Products and Services team is the driving force behind Concentrix’s transformation, data, and technology services. We integrate world-class digital engineering, creativity, and a deep understanding of human behavior to find and unlock value through tech-powered and intelligence-fueled experiences. We combine human-centered design, powerful data, and strong tech to accelerate transformation at scale. You will be surrounded by the best in the world providing market leading technology and insights to modernize and simplify the customer experience. Within our professional services team, you will deliver strategic consulting, design, advisory services, market research, and contact center analytics that deliver insights to improve outcomes and value for our clients. Hence achieving our vision.

Our game-changers around the world have devoted their careers to ensuring every relationship is exceptional. And we’re proud to be recognized with awards such as "World's Best Workplaces," “Best Companies for Career Growth,” and “Best Company Culture,” year after year.

Join us and be part of this journey towards greater opportunities and brighter futures.

Job Summary

We are seeking a Marketing Data Analyst to support data-driven marketing initiatives by transforming complex datasets into actionable business insights. This role will work closely with clients and internal teams to analyze campaign performance, identify customer behavior trends, and develop presentations that clearly communicate findings and recommendations. The ideal candidate has hands-on experience with SQL or SAS, marketing analytics, and creating client-facing reports and PowerPoint presentations.

Responsibilities


 Develop analytical solutions and reporting strategies to support client marketing initiatives and business objectives
 Perform quantitative analysis, customer segmentation, predictive modeling, and campaign performance measurement using SQL, SAS, or similar tools
 Analyze large datasets from multiple sources to identify trends, insights, and opportunities for campaign optimization
 Create client-facing presentations in PowerPoint and translate analytical findings into clear business recommendations
 Partner with Program Management and IT teams to support data architecture analysis and improve marketing program effectiveness


Qualifications


 2+ years of experience in marketing analytics, business analytics, or data analysis roles
 Hands-on experience with SQL and/or SAS for data extraction, analysis, and reporting
 Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint, including development of executive-level presentations
 Experience with statistical analysis, regression modeling, segmentation, testing methodologies, and campaign measurement
 Experience with Salesforce, Einstein Analytics, Datorama, Snowflake, Oracle, or SQL Server is preferred
 Bachelor’s degree in Analytics, Statistics, Marketing, Business, Computer Science, or a related field

description:
About the job

Canonical is a leading provider of open source software and operating systems to the global enterprise and technology markets. Our platform, Ubuntu, is very widely used in breakthrough enterprise initiatives such as public cloud, data science, AI, engineering innovation, and IoT. Our customers include the world's leading public cloud and silicon providers, and industry leaders in many sectors. The company is a pioneer of global distributed collaboration, with 1200+ colleagues in 75+ countries and very few office-based roles. Teams meet two to four times yearly in person, in interesting locations around the world, to align on strategy and execution.

The company is founder-led, profitable, and growing.

Open Role: Graduate Partner Marketing Manager

Marketers at Canonical are brand stewards and demand creators.They transform complex open-source technology into messages, stories and strategies that shape the market understanding of our vision. Some focus on differentiated product and portfolio positioning; others specialize in account-based engagement, partner and alliance messaging, or product-led growth. Whatever their specialization, each marketer works as part of a cross-functional unit, contributing campaigns and insights that help the company learn and grow faster.

We have a bias for action, focus efforts on the most impactful activities and take ownership of our results. We work across long buying cycles, balance storytelling with data, design experiments, track outcomes, and iterate rapidly to find what works.

Marketers at Canonical don’t just promote open source; they amplify it, ensuring our innovations reach the right people, at the right time, with clarity and purpose from developers to decision makers.

As a Graduate Partner Marketing Manager, you aren’t just running campaigns; you are architecting the strategic relationships that drive our business forward. You will collaborate with the titans of tech:


Public cloud: AWS, Google, Microsoft, Oracle.
Silicon: NVIDIA, Intel, Arm, Qualcomm, AMD.
Global hardware: Dell, HP, Lenovo.



Early careers & growth

We are specifically looking for recent graduates who are serious about their success and ready to make a global impact. At Canonical, we prioritize talent and potential over years of experience; we will teach you the technical and commercial nuances of the open-source world through our structured career journey program. You will gain experience working with top tier talent and with multiple enterprise technologies. If you have the drive and a high standard for excellence, we provide the platform for you to become a world-class marketing leader.

Location: This role will be based remotely in the Americas region and will report to the Partner Marketing Team Manager.

Your impact


Architect joint success: Build high-velocity go-to-market plans with global partners, moving from initial messaging to full-scale pipeline generation.
Launch at scale: Lead integrated campaigns, industry announcements, and product launches that reach everyone from developers to C-suite decision-makers.
Creative packaging: Translate cutting-edge tech in the open-source world into educational and marketing content that resonates.
Ecosystem evolution: Shape and scale our partner programs to keep pace with an ever-expanding network of hardware vendors, software vendors, and channel resellers.



What We’re Looking For


The marketing basics: You understand how marketing works (especially within enterprises) and have familiarity with common marketing platforms, social media, and business technology.
The relationship builder: You can navigate complex and dynamic organizations and build lasting rapport.
The analytical thinker: You obsess over value and use data to decide which experiments to scale and which to iterate.
The self-starter: You thrive in a fast-paced, remote environment and aren't afraid to "roll up your sleeves" to get a project across the finish line.



What We Offer You

Your base pay will depend on various factors including your geographical location, level of experience, knowledge and skills. In addition to the benefits above, certain roles are also eligible for additional benefits and rewards including annual bonuses and sales incentives based on revenue or utilisation. Our compensation philosophy is to ensure equity right across our global workforce.

In addition to a competitive base pay, we provide all team members with additional benefits, which reflect our values and ideals. Please note that additional benefits may apply depending on the work location and, for more information on these, please ask your Talent Partner.


Fully remote working environment - we’ve been working remotely since 2004!
Personal learning and development budget of 2,000USD per annum
Annual compensation review
Recognition rewards
Annual holiday leave
Parental Leave
Employee Assistance Programme
Opportunity to travel to new locations to meet colleagues at ‘sprints’
Priority Pass for travel and travel upgrades for long haul company events



About Canonical

Canonical is a pioneering tech firm that is at the forefront of the global move to open source. As the company that publishes Ubuntu, one of the most important open source projects and the platform for AI, IoT and the cloud, we are changing the world on a daily basis. We recruit on a global basis and set a very high standard for people joining the company. We expect excellence - in order to succeed, we need to be the best at what we do.

Canonical has been a remote-first company since its inception in 2004. Work at Canonical is a step into the future, and will challenge you to think differently, work smarter, learn new skills, and raise your game. Canonical provides a unique window into the world of 21st-century digital business.

Canonical is an equal opportunity employer

We are proud to foster a workplace free from discrimination. Diversity of experience, perspectives, and background create a better work environment and better products. Whatever your identity, we will give your application fair consideration.

description:
About the job

Job Description

Are you an ecommerce expert and keen to shape the way LEGO® employs digital platforms in emerging markets? Bring your ecommerce experience to play in creating the world class online shopper experiences. Be a hands-on leader in the LATAM Team to help steer the LATAM B2B eCommerce team, creating an innovative and learning team culture! In this role, you will acquire knowledge of shopping behavior and the retail landscape across all B2B platforms. These include web, mobile, applications, and physical locations. You will apply this knowledge to help drive digital strategy and complete digital solutions for strategic customers across retail channels.

NOTE All resumes must be submitted in English to be considered. 


Core Responsibilities

E-Commerce Strategy and Planning





Ensure that the LATAM eComm channel opportunities are well reflected in the Go to Market plans and Customer Business Plans.
Leading, finding opportunities and driving growth of the eComm channel in LATAM focusing on strategic priorities.
Input into LEGO global / AMS eComm direction and convert into actionable local initiatives.
Identify emerging eComm opportunities and bring them to life.
Drive collaborations with LATAM sales team and cross functional teams to ensure broad strategy alignment is included in the digital execution across various account plans.
Key partner for GM in eComm related daily business tasks.
Lead best in class digital merchandising executions with B2B clients




For strategic customers





Apply understanding of retailer’s digital strategies in conjunction with LEGO strategy to agree annual joint digital plans as part of the joint business plan (including test & learn).
Create and maintain a commercially focused digital plan to ensure maximum exposure and conversion.
Lead relationships with all eComm customers and serve as senior customer point of contact.
Lead account strategy, including discussions, innovations and testing.




Execution and Evaluation





Bring brands to life through execution and management of Go-To-Market initiatives directly working with key departments within our selected retailers to build and activate plans.
Project manager execution of plans in collaboration with sales, marketing ensuring clear organizational alignment for efficient execution (RAID).
Implement digital initiatives within customers ensuring seamless experience across channels (online, mobile, app, store).
Evaluate and share post activity results with recommendations both internally and externally in order to drive continuous improvement and embed findings with sales team.
Accountable for coordinating campaigns & activities across LATAM eComm customers.




People Leadership





Lead a team of 2.
Work in close collaboration with the Digital Marketing and Trade Marketing colleagues and also drive capability building across the LATAM organization.
Coordinate eComm team resources to provide effective support to all required customers with e-Comm sales and alignment of those plans across geographies.
Facilitate talent development and retention of digital workforce within BU, building a strong and collaborative work environment - Build a test and learn culture to encourage innovation and experimentation - Lead and develop capabilities for eCommerce team




Do your part in our team succeeding

Establishing an ecommerce strategy and its implementation is key growth driver for LEGO in LATAM. Ecommerce team is expected to run all the B2B ecommerce activations across Mexico and Brazil markets with best-in-class quality by collaborating with other functions e.g. Marketing, Sales, Operations, Distributors, Enablers, Regional team to deliver harmonised brand experience across touch points.

Do you have what it takes?





Demonstrated advanced experience in ecommerce and/or other digital marketing prowess ideally from a premium consumer brand or digital agency with a few years in a people leadership capacity
Fully operational in selling and negotiation skills, sales & commercial analysis and customer collaboration.
First-hand experience with LATAM ecommerce platforms - either from brand principal’s side or vendor/merchant management side.
Full understanding of the conversion funnel, the digital retail landscape, and the dynamics of ecommerce and omni-channel, as well as thorough knowledge of new shopper acquisition channels
Commercially and strong persuading skills
A proven track-record of developing and implementing digital solutions or campaigns based on shopper insights
Excellent analytical skills, a passion for data metrics and turning analysis into the creation and execution of actionable e-commerce programs and digital campaigns
Digitally fluent, including the ability to understand key technical issues and their implications
Strong networking skills and interpersonal skills
Ability to think strategically, but also have a “hands-on” approach




Relocation

There is no relocation budget for this role.

Hybrid working model

Our workplace enables our LEGO colleagues to be and do their best at work. Introducing a flexible way of working is a great example of how we live up to our ambition. This 3 day in the office hybrid model will exemplify our People Promise by embracing the different life situations of our colleagues.

Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis. However, please note we do amend or withdraw our jobs and reserve the right to do so at any time, including prior to any advertised closing date. So, if you're interested in this role we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

What’s in it for you? 

Here are some of what to expect

Family Care Leave – We offer enhanced paid leave options for those important times.

Insurances – All colleagues are covered by our life and disability insurance which provides protection and peace of mind.

Wellbeing – We want you to be your best self, so you’ll have access to the Headspace App and lots of wellbeing initiatives and programs run by local teams where you are based

Colleague Discount – We know you'll love to build so from day 1 you will qualify for our generous colleague discount.

Bonus – We do our best work to succeed together. When goals are reached and if eligible, you'll be rewarded through our bonus scheme

Your workplace – You'll find this at the top of this advert and when you join the team, we'll confirm this with you.

We strive to create a diverse, dynamic and inclusive culture of play at the LEGO Group, where everyone feels safe, valued and they belong.

The LEGO Group is highly committed to equal employment opportunity and equal pay and seeks to encourage applicants from all backgrounds (eg. sex, gender identity or expression, race/ethnicity, national origin, sexual orientation, disability, age, religion and Veteran status) to apply for roles in our team.

We support our employees in being there for the moments that matter in life and celebrate families of all kinds, the loved ones that make us who we are. Being part of the LEGO Group also means taking part in our annual Play Day, playing a part in building a sustainable future and continuing our mission to “inspire and develop the builders of tomorrow.”

The LEGO Group is fully committed to Children’s Rights and Child Wellbeing across the globe. Candidates offered positions with high engagement with children are required to take part in Child Safeguarding Background Screening, as a condition of the offer.

Thank you for sharing our global commitment to Children’s Rights.

We conduct drug screening as a part of our drug free workplace policy and in support of our commitment to the health and safety of our employees.

Online Application Accessibility Statement; which is intended for people with disabilities - LEGO systems endeavors to make www.LEGO.com/jobs accessible to any and all users. If you would like to contact us regarding the accessibility of our web site or need assistance completing the application process, please contact the HR Service Desk at 1.860-763-7777, option #3. Please note, these communication channels should be used for those having difficulty accessing our on-line channels, not to inquire about job opportunities.

Just imagine building your dream career.

Then make it real.

Join the LEGO® team today.

description:
About the job

Condé Nast is a global media company producing the highest quality content with a footprint of more than 1 billion consumers in 32 territories through print, digital, video and social platforms. The company’s portfolio includes many of the world’s most respected and influential media properties including Vogue, Vanity Fair, Glamour, Self, GQ, The New Yorker, Condé Nast Traveler/Traveller, Allure, AD, Bon Appétit and Wired, among others.

Job Description

Location:

Ciudad de México, CMX

Condé Nast is a global media company, home to iconic brands including Vogue, The New Yorker, GQ, Glamour, AD, Vanity Fair and Wired, among many others. The company's award-winning content reaches 84 million consumers in print, 367 million in digital and 379 million across social platforms, and generates more than 1 billion video views each month.

The company is headquartered in London and New York, and operates in 32 markets worldwide, including China, France, Germany, India, Italy, Japan, Mexico & Latin America, Spain, Taiwan, the UK and the USA, with local license partners across the globe.

What we’re looking for:

A Workday Specialist to support the critical work of maintaining and evolving our Workday platform within a full tenant implementation (Finance, U.S. Payroll and HCM). You will work within a broader Workday team that supports key business objectives by working with business stakeholders, project leads, and cross functional technology teams to build, test and deliver key technology efforts. The ideal candidate has strong communication and interpersonal skills and enjoys working in a dynamic, global, team-oriented environment along with keen attention to detail and the ability to collaborate with a diverse group of stakeholders across various business functions.

Responsibilities include, but are not limited to:


General understanding of Workday security
Foundational knowledge to configure Workday business processes
Ability to develop and maintain standard Workday reports
Partner with stakeholders and senior Workday Specialists to understand requirements and ensure results meet the business needs
Leverage knowledge of customer support principals, and best practices to provide best in class service to our internal customers
Engage with vendor support as needed for support related to the platform
Prioritize work against priorities and dependencies using team processes and standards
Work with product managers and senior specialists to review Workday business processes, and evaluate against new features and functionality with an eye to increased automation and reduction of manual process
Support new Workday release activities relating to the needs of the business and impact assessment


Essential Skills & Requirements:


1+ years supporting Workday or similar experiences in other ERPs
Ability to take instruction, prioritize and execute tasks in a dynamic, flexible environment
Foundational knowledge with ticketing systems and enterprise collaboration tools
Strong interpersonal skills and customer service orientation
Highly self-motivated, self-directed, and attentive to detail
Ability to work with cross-functional teams


What happens next?

If you are interested in this opportunity, please apply below, and we will review your application as soon as possible. You can update your resume or upload a cover letter at any time by accessing your candidate profile.

Condé Nast is an equal opportunity employer. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran status, age, familial status and other legally protected characteristics.

title: Digital Content Creator
skills: Demonstrated experience creating digital content (e.g., social posts, short-form copy, and campaign assets) for diverse audiences and platforms.

Strong writing, recording, editing, and proofreading skills, with attention to detail, tone, and consistency in English; Spanish proficiency is highly beneficial.
Familiarity with social media platforms, content management systems, and basic analytics tools to optimize content based on performance data.
Comfort working in a hybrid environment, managing deadlines, and coordinating with cross-functional teams in a fast-paced, creative setting.

description:
About the job

Company Description VML is a global creative and marketing agency formed from the unification of Wunderman Thompson and VMLY&R, combining complementary capabilities and geographic strengths. The organization delivers fully integrated solutions across brand experience, customer experience, and commerce, grounded in strong data and technology expertise. Teams collaborate across regions to support a diverse portfolio of international clients and industries. VML offers opportunities to work with cutting-edge tools, multidisciplinary teams, and innovative campaigns. For more information, visit www.vml.com.




Role Description This full-time Digital Content Creator role is based in Mexico City, Mexico and follows a hybrid work model, combining on-site collaboration with flexible work-from-home days. In this role, the Digital Content Creator develops engaging content for digital platforms, including social media and campaigns, aligned with brand guidelines and strategic goals. Responsibilities include planning content calendars, drafting and adapting copy, visual asset creation, and optimizing content for performance and engagement. The role also involves collaborating closely with creative, strategy, and account teams to ensure consistency across channels, monitoring content performance metrics, and iterating based on insights and feedback. The Digital Content Creator is expected to stay current with digital trends, platform best practices, and emerging formats to help evolve VML’s content approach.




Qualifications

Demonstrated experience creating digital content (e.g., social posts, short-form copy, and campaign assets) for diverse audiences and platforms.
Ability to translate brand and campaign strategies into clear, compelling narratives and visual concepts in collaboration with designers and strategists.
Strong writing, recording, editing, and proofreading skills, with attention to detail, tone, and consistency in English; Spanish proficiency is highly beneficial.
Familiarity with social media platforms, content management systems, and basic analytics tools to optimize content based on performance data.
Comfort working in a hybrid environment, managing deadlines, and coordinating with cross-functional teams in a fast-paced, creative setting.
Understanding of digital marketing fundamentals, including audience segmentation, engagement tactics, and creative best practices.
Prior experience in a creative agency or digital marketing environment is a plus.
Bachelor’s degree in Communications, Marketing, Design, or a related field, or equivalent practical experience.

description:
About the job

Acuity Inc. (NYSE: AYI) is a market-leading industrial technology company. We use technology to solve problems in spaces, light and more things to come. Through our two business segments, Acuity Brands Lighting (ABL) and Acuity Intelligent Spaces (AIS), we design, manufacture, and bring to market products and services that make a valuable difference in people’s lives.

We achieve growth through the development of innovative new products and services, including lighting, lighting controls, building management solutions, and an audio, video and control platform. We focus on customer outcomes and drive growth and productivity to increase market share and deliver superior returns. We look to aggressively deploy capital to grow the business and to enter attractive new verticals.

Acuity Inc. is based in Atlanta, Georgia, with operations across North America, Europe and Asia. The Company is powered by approximately 13,000 dedicated and talented associates. Visit us at www.acuityinc.com .

Work location:


 This position may be based anywhere in the United States and includes travel as part of the responsibilities. 
 This position requires on-site presence in M2, Guadalupe, Nuevo Leon, Mexico, following a hybrid work model. 
 This position requires on-site presence in M2, Guadalupe, Nuevo Leon, Mexico, must report to the office every business day. 


Job Summary

Responsible for supporting all channel customers by providing exemplary proactive order management support. Responsibilities may be, but not limited to: order entry, simple to difficult order and project management, intake of all customer inquiries and resolution ownership of any task, regardless of domain. Candidate should continually seek opportunities to broaden customer relationships by ensuring customer requirements and expectations are exceeded. Candidate should expect to become an expert capable of training and teaching others. Expect to be responsible for documenting knowledge and process.

Key Tasks & Responsibilities (Essential Functions)

Order Management

Intake

Company Initiatives


 Active contributor in annual initiatives to increase the industry service level 
 Active contributor in annual initiatives to improve team productivity

 Bachelor Degree

0-25%

Acuity offers generous benefits including health care, dental coverage, vision plans, 401K benefits, and commissions/incentive compensation depending on the role. title: Consumer Operations Associate
skills: Troubleshoot and solve external user issues
Analyze our processes and instigate changes to help scale our operations and improve user experience
Work closely with other Stripe teams to improve our product offerings and messaging around new products and features
Gain unique insights into how thousands of businesses on Stripe scale and operate
Have an opportunity to learn how Stripe operates from the ground up and make a real impact on the future of the organization
Be a part of building a brand-new team and operational culture for Stripe
Partner with global operations specialists or business process owners to run a critical workflow
You have a user-first mindset and are energized by the challenge of solving difficult problems
You have excellent communication skills, both written and verbal
You excel in critical thinking and problem solving
You have prior experience in customer service or internal and external user-facing operations
You enjoy learning about technical products and processes, you're an organized, self-starter with passion for finding solutions to our user’s challenges
You have a process-oriented mindset and the ability to get things done
You enjoy working in an in-office environment with strong cross-team collaboration and support
You prioritize well and enjoy working in a quick-moving environment
You are humble and have a proven track record for working well across teams and with external partners
You're willing to periodically work a weekend day for which you'll receive a weekday off. The SDC operates during daytime hours with shifts from the morning through early evening
Prior experience or knowledge in user support
Prior experience working on projects or process improvement initiatives
description:
About the job

Who we are

About Stripe

Stripe is a financial infrastructure platform for businesses. Millions of companies—from the world’s largest enterprises to the most ambitious startups—use Stripe to accept payments, grow their revenue, and accelerate new business opportunities. Our mission is to increase the GDP of the internet, and we have a staggering amount of work ahead. That means you have an unprecedented opportunity to put the global economy within everyone’s reach while doing the most important work of your career.

About The Team

At Stripe, our Mexico City office is a vibrant hub at the forefront of our mission to reshape the financial landscape for businesses worldwide. Our team prioritizes collaboration, innovation, and excellence. As a member of the Mexico City team, you'll be part of a mission-driven community dedicated to enhancing the global economy and increasing the GDP of the internet. We strive for excellence by creating with craft and beauty, while having fun and celebrating our successes together.

We cultivate a culture of collaboration, inclusivity, and support where every team member’s voice matters. Our commitment shines through as we handle over a million support cases each year, empowering our users not just to solve problems but to achieve their goals.

What you’ll do

Responsibilities


Troubleshoot and solve external user issues 
Analyze our processes and instigate changes to help scale our operations and improve user experience 
Work closely with other Stripe teams to improve our product offerings and messaging around new products and features 
Gain unique insights into how thousands of businesses on Stripe scale and operate 
Have an opportunity to learn how Stripe operates from the ground up and make a real impact on the future of the organization 
Be a part of building a brand-new team and operational culture for Stripe 
Partner with global operations specialists or business process owners to run a critical workflow



Who you are

We’re looking for someone who meets the minimum requirements to be considered for the role. If you meet these requirements, you are encouraged to apply. The preferred qualifications are a bonus, not a requirement.

Minimum Requirements


You have a user-first mindset and are energized by the challenge of solving difficult problems
You have excellent communication skills, both written and verbal
You excel in critical thinking and problem solving
You have prior experience in customer service or internal and external user-facing operations
You enjoy learning about technical products and processes, you're an organized, self-starter with passion for finding solutions to our user’s challenges
You have a process-oriented mindset and the ability to get things done
You enjoy working in an in-office environment with strong cross-team collaboration and support
You prioritize well and enjoy working in a quick-moving environment
You are humble and have a proven track record for working well across teams and with external partners
You're willing to periodically work a weekend day for which you'll receive a weekday off. The SDC operates during daytime hours with shifts from the morning through early evening



Preferred Qualifications


Prior experience or knowledge in user support 
Prior experience working on projects or process improvement initiatives 



In-office expectations

Office-assigned Stripes in most of our locations are currently expected to spend at least 50% of the time in a given month in their local office or with users. This expectation may vary depending on role, team and location. For example, Stripes in Stripe Delivery Center roles in Mexico City, Mexico, Bengaluru, India, and Dublin, Ireland work 100% from the office. Also, some teams have greater in-office attendance requirements, to appropriately support our users and workflows, which the hiring manager will discuss. This approach helps strike a balance between bringing people together for in-person collaboration and learning from each other, while supporting flexibility when possible.

Pay and benefits

Stripe does not yet include pay ranges in job postings in every country. Stripe strongly values pay transparency and is working toward pay transparency globally.

description:
About the job

Overview

CAREERS TO SMILE ABOUT 

At PepsiCo, you’ll discover a place where our mission is to create smiles around the world. With a portfolio of more than 500 beloved brands including, Gatorade, Lay’s and Quaker, our work touches millions of people every day.

At the heart of the company is a team of thinkers, creators, and problem-solvers who collaborate to innovate and turn ideas into action. Driven by innovation and a focus on creating joyful moments through food and drinks, our decisions are guided by consumer centricity, creating opportunities for our associates to do meaningful work and make a lasting impact in the communities we serve.

Whatever your role, you’ll be part of a global community that values your ideas and empowers you to make an impact, on your career and on the world around you.

Responsibilities

The Opportunity 

As a One Touch & Process Automation Analyst, the candidate will gain in-depth exposure to end-to-end purchasing operations for beverage products while supporting the U.S. beverage market. The position offers a unique opportunity to contribute to key operational processes, including invoice validation and Proof of Delivery (POD) reviews, while driving impactful process improvement initiatives.

This is an excellent opportunity for someone passionate about automation, data analysis, and continuous improvement. The selected candidate will play a key role in identifying inefficiencies, developing automation solutions, and enhancing daily operational processes that directly support business performance.

Your Impact 


Develop and implement automation solutions to improve purchasing processes and operational efficiency.
Manage inventory records and maintain purchasing and inventory reports to support business decisions.
Review invoices and Proof of Delivery (POD) documentation to ensure accuracy and compliance.
Investigate and resolve delivery discrepancies by coordinating with carriers and Customer Care teams.
Identify opportunities for process improvement and support continuous improvement initiatives across purchasing operations.


Qualifications

Who Are We Looking For? 


Experience in process improvement and operational efficiency initiatives.
Fluent English (written and spoken).
Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, particularly Excel, Outlook, and PowerPoint.
Knowledge of Microsoft Power Platform (Power BI, Power Apps, Power Automate, and Power Pages).
Ability to learn and navigate multiple systems and platforms.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
Proactive, curious, and self-driven mindset.
Excellent teamwork and communication skills.
Knowledge of programming languages or scripting tools is a plus, but not required.


If this is an opportunity that interests you, we encourage you to apply even if you do not meet 100% of the requirements.

What can you expect from us: 


Opportunities to learn and develop every day through a wide range of programs. 
Internal digital platforms that promote self-learning. 
Development programs according to Leadership skills. 
Specialized training according to the role. 
Learning experiences with internal and external providers. 
We love to celebrate success, which is why we have recognition programs for seniority, behavior, leadership, moments of life, among others. 
Financial wellness programs that will help you reach your goals in all stages of life. 
A flexibility program that will allow you to balance your personal and work life, adapting your working day to your lifestyle. 
And because your family is also important to us, they can also enjoy benefits such as our Wellness Line, thousands of Agreements and Discounts, Scholarship programs for your children, Aid Plans for different moments of life, among others. 


We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. We respect and value diversity as a work force and innovation for the organization.

description:
